$Q(n)$
is a statement parameterized by a positive integer
$n$
. The following theorem is proven by induction:
Theorem: For any positive integer
$n$
,
$Q(n)$
is true.
What must be proven in the inductive step?

$g_0 = 1$

$g_n = 3 \cdot g_{n - 1} + 2n$
, for
$n \ge 1$
.
If the theorem below is proven by induction, what must be established in the inductive step?
Theorem: For any nonnegative integer
$n$
,
$g_n =  rac 5 2 \cdot 2^n -n-  rac 3 2$
.

Select the mathematical statements to correctly fill in the beginning of the proof of an inductive step below:
We will assume for
$k \ge 1$
that
$4$
divides
$9^k - 5^k$
and will prove that
$4$
divides
$9^{k+1}-5^{k+1}$
. Since, by the inductive hypothesis,
$4$
divides
$9^k - 5^k$
, then
$9^k$
can be expressed as (
$A$
?), where
$m$
is an integer.
$$ egin{array}{lll}
9^{k+1}-5^{k+1} & =  9 \cdot 9^k - 5 \cdot 5^k\
9^{k+1}-5^{k+1} & =  (B?)  &~\mbox{By the ind. Hyp.}\
9^{k+1}-5^{k+1} & =  \cdots 
\end{array}$$

The sequence
$\{ h_n\}$
is defined recursively as follows:
$h_0 = 1$
, and
$h_n = 3 \cdot h_{n - 1} + 1$
, for
$n \ge 1$
.
Suppose that the following theorem is proven by induction:
Theorem: For any nonnegative integer
$n$
,
$h_n =  rac 1 2 \cdot (3^{n+1} -1)$
.
The proof of the induction step (after stating what is assumed and will be proven) starts out as:
$$ egin{array}{lll}
h_{k+1} & =  3 \cdot h_k + 1 &~(\mbox{Label}~1)\
h_{k+1} & =  3  \cdot  rac 1 2 (3^{k+1}-1)  &~(\mbox{Label}~2)\
h_{k+1} & =  \cdots 
\end{array}$$
What are the correct labels for lines
$1$
and
$2$
?
